# Long horizon — status: minor_revision

**Posture.** 5+ year stance: the household is ALREADY diversified across 8 asset classes (Core Equity 26.2%, Dividend 18.3%, Individual Stocks 18.2% with NVDA ~18% strategic, Cash 12.9%, Defensive 11.1%, Growth 10.9%, International 2.4%, Alternative 0%) and is on paper FI under a 3.5% SWR (11.2M NIS × 0.035 = 392k > 277k canonical spend). The work is NOT a transition from a single concentrated position — that framing was incorrect in synth #19 and is retracted. The work IS: (a) reduce NVDA from its RSU-driven lot peaks toward a 15% strategic cap by 2031, (b) migrate residual US-domiciled non-NVDA holdings to UCITS to drive the US-situs estate tail from $1.37M toward <$1M, (c) accumulate ~5 more years of salary+RSU+ESPP toward the 22M NIS / age-49 target while preserving capital-preservation drawdown discipline, (d) close the will/POA/guardianship gap. The plan's 3.5% SWR framing is retained as the headline 'FI achieved' lens while capital-preservation (2.4% real yield) is the binding decision rule, and the gap between the two is acknowledged rather than papered over.

## Targets
- **Gross long-horizon FI base (2031 target, returns-only basis)**: 22000000.0 nis (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — 22M NIS 2031 target from the user's plan baseline (Jacobs_Wealth_Plan). Reaffirmed against 277k canonical spend with a buffer band above the 11.54M NIS bare-FI threshold. Achievable with current ~11.2M base + ~360k NIS/yr bank-side savings + ~$315k/yr net RSU accumulation over 5 years.
- **Education carve-out (combined household contribution, 2 kids)**: 1000000.0 nis (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — 500k NIS per child × 2 (Adva, Geva); codified in prior cycle and preserved.
- **Net FI-eligible base (gross minus education carve-out)**: 21000000.0 nis (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — Gross 22M NIS minus 1M NIS education carve-out = 21M NIS net base against which capital-preservation FI is measured.
- **Required real yield floor on net FI base**: 0.0132 ratio (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — 277,004 NIS canonical spend / 21,000,000 NIS net base = 1.32% real yield floor — well below the plan's 4.5% real-return assumption, leaving headroom for multiple-compression and FX drag.
- **Bare-FI threshold at 2.4% real yield (returns-only, 277k spend)**: 11540000.0 nis (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — 277,004 / 0.024 ≈ 11.54M NIS — bare returns-only FI under capital-preservation. Current ~11.2M leaves only ~300k NIS margin (~2.7% buffer) vs the plan's 3.5% SWR framing that implies 3.3M cushion. Acknowledged tighter under user's elected drawdown style.
- **Info-tech sector cap on MANAGED PORTFOLIO (excludes NVDA RSU income stream)**: 0.35 pct_of_portfolio (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — 35% sector cap on managed portfolio; NVDA RSU income-stream lots excluded from denominator per architectural decision. Tech-sector glidepath question remains open with user.
- **US-situs new-position risk-acceptance ceiling**: 1500000.0 usd (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — Risk-acceptance ceiling on NEW US-situs positions, not a convergence target on existing stack. Existing US-situs ~$3.09M is being migrated via UCITS-first deployment.
- **US-situs estate tail (current exposure estimate)**: 1370000.0 usd (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — Accepted prior-round delta preserved. 40% × ($3.5M US-situs aggregate − $60k NRA exemption) ≈ $1.37M. PlanCritique snapshot-based recomputation today shows ~$1.21M; the $1.37M target retains a slight safety overhead pending Atlanta SFR / incidental items reconciliation.
- **NVDA strategic share of portfolio (2031 target)**: 15.0 pct_of_portfolio (stated 2026-06-02; revisit 2027-06-01) — From RSU-driven lot peaks (snapshot 11,471 sh ≈ 64.9% of $3,540k tradeable book, but ~18% strategic per user's 8-asset-class diversification reading) toward 15% strategic by 2031 via the 3,000 sh/yr cadence.

## Themes
- **Portfolio is already diversified — work is NVDA-cap reduction + UCITS migration + 5-year accumulation, NOT a de-risking transition** (lean_into) — Snapshot supports 8-asset-class diversification (Core Equity 26.2%, Dividend 18.3%, Individual Stocks 18.2%, Cash 12.9%, Defensive 11.1%, Growth 10.9%, International 2.4%, Alternative 0%) per user directive. NVDA strategic share is ~18%, not 61%; the 61% figure reflected an RSU lot peak. Prior synth #19's 'single concentrated stock to durable portfolio' framing was wrong and is retracted.
- **Capital-preservation / returns-only drawdown is binding decision rule; 3.5% SWR remains documentary headline** (lean_into) — User has elected capital_preservation_returns_only; bare-FI threshold under that rule is 11.54M NIS, materially tighter than the plan's 3.5% SWR 'cleared by 3.3M' framing. Both views are tracked so the decision rule (capital preservation) governs while the headline (FI achieved on paper) remains intact.
- **UCITS-first deployment is the binding architectural choice for new US-equity exposure** (lean_into) — Non-US-person status + US-situs estate exposure makes Ireland-domiciled UCITS (CSPX, ACWD, FWRA, CNDX) the only durable vehicle for new US-equity exposure. Confirmed GREEN by PlanCritique; dividend WHT economics roughly neutral under the US-Israel treaty.
- **Estate-documents gap (will, POA, healthcare directive, guardianship) is a top-three structural risk** (lean_into) — will_exists=false, no POA, no healthcare directive, no guardianship designation for 2 minors aged 6 and 10 across IL/RO/US. Form 706-NA filing burden unavoidable on heirs while US-situs >$60k. Independent of UCITS migration pace.
- **Education 1M NIS carve-out is netted; 1.5M NIS per-child apartment goal is kid-side aspirational, not a household liability** (monitor) — Distinction preserved from prior cycle: education funding is a household obligation netted against the FI base; apartment goal is the kid's target, tracked but not deducted.
- **Broad-market multiple-compression risk on the 4.5% real-return assumption** (monitor) — MacroAnalyst regime=neutral (10Y 4.45%, WTI $97.63 stagflation drag); fleet-level multiples stretched outside NVDA (TSLA PEG 31 on -39% earnings growth, AMD PEG 3.07, AMZN EV/EBITDA 115). Accepted prior-round delta preserved; treat as parameterized stress on plan's 4.5% real-return baseline, not base case.
- **Tech-sector cap glidepath — user counter-position remains unresolved** (monitor) — Concern about info-tech sector concentration on the managed-portfolio denominator (excluding NVDA RSU stream) remains open; last user DISAGREE response addressed the wrong item.

## Actions
- **Continue NVDA single-name deconcentration toward 15% strategic by 2031**: Maintain the 3,000 sh/yr cadence (user-confirmed; intake nvda_sale_progress.target_shares=3,000). At constant price, share-count glide: 11,471 → ~8,471 (end-2026) → ~5,471 (end-2027) → ~2,471 (end-2028) → 15% strategic by 2031. — Aligns realized cadence with the user's stated 15% 2031 strategic cap and avoids surtax-band acceleration that the plan's legacy 10,000 sh/yr 2026 schedule would force.
- **Migrate residual US-domiciled non-NVDA holdings to UCITS over 24 months**: Phase-out of SCHD, SCHG, VOO, QQQM, SPMO, VTV, SGOV and similar US-domiciled wrappers in favor of CSPX / ACWD / FWRA / CNDX (Irish-domiciled, foreign-corp shares, not US-situs). Target: drive US-situs estate tail from $1.37M toward <$1M by end-2028. — Materially reduces 706-NA filing burden on heirs and Form 706-NA 40%-above-$60k exposure; dividend economics roughly neutral under treaty.
- **Execute estate-documentation program (will, guardianship, POA, healthcare directive, living trust)**: Engage cross-border (IL/RO/US) attorney; draft will addressing each jurisdiction's intestate regime; designate guardianship for Adva and Geva; execute POA and healthcare directive; decide on living trust for minor beneficiaries. — PlanCritique RED on estate-documents gap; will_exists=false with 2 minor children and tri-jurisdictional assets is asymmetric tail risk independent of UCITS migration pace.
- **Codify gross/net FI split and capital-preservation framing in goals_yaml**: Persist gross 22M NIS, education 1M NIS carve-out, net 21M NIS, 277k NIS spend, 2.4% real yield floor, and 11.54M NIS bare-FI threshold as structured fields in goals_yaml; flag plan's 3.5% SWR as documentary, capital-preservation as binding. — Prevents recurrence of the 500k NIS spend / 10.3M NIS FI drift PlanCritique flagged in prior versions; gives downstream agents a canonical source of truth.
- **Re-route tech-sector glidepath question to user in next cycle**: Surface the unrefuted tech-sector concentration concern (managed-portfolio denominator, excluding NVDA RSU) explicitly and separately from the NVDA RSU question. — Last DISAGREE addressed wrong item; concern remains open and requires user judgment.
- **Re-validate 22M NIS gross midpoint at next annual PlanCritique cycle**: Treat 22M NIS as a working midpoint; re-derive against 277k spend × buffer band each annual cycle. — Annual freshness convention for long horizon.

## Deltas vs. prior current
- [added] Add explicit 'already-diversified across 8 asset classes' theme; retract prior synth #19's incorrect 'single concentrated stock to durable portfolio' framing. (theme `long.themes.portfolio_already_diversified_8_asset_classes`)
- [added] Re-add explicit NVDA strategic 15% target by 2031 (lineage to prior draft #18 target same id). (target `long.targets.single_name_concentration_cap__long_term`)

## Rationale
Long horizon remains intentionally sticky on substrate (22M NIS gross / 21M net FI base, 1.32% real yield floor, 11.54M bare-FI threshold, $1.37M US-situs tail, UCITS-first architecture, NVDA 15% strategic by 2031, education carve-out, estate-documents gap as top-three risk). The minor revision this cycle is two-fold: (a) retract the incorrect 'single concentrated stock to durable portfolio' framing — the household is already diversified across 8 asset classes per the user directive and the snapshot, with NVDA at ~18% strategic, not 61% — and re-frame the long-horizon work as NVDA-cap reduction + UCITS migration + 5-year accumulation; (b) re-anchor the NVDA strategic 15% target explicitly into the long-horizon target stack (reusing prior draft #18's item_id since intent is identical). Capital-preservation remains the binding decision rule with 3.5% SWR retained as documentary headline. Confidence MEDIUM: macro/FX/multiples data are LIVE but the plan's 4.5% real-return assumption needs explicit multiple-compression-stress treatment, and tax-substrate domain files still carry last_verified=1900-01-01 pending domain-refresh.
